title: No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion and Abortion Insurance Full Disclosure Act of 2025
short_title: No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion and Abortion Insurance Full Disclosure Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill makes permanent rules that stop the federal government from using taxpayer money to pay for abortions or health insurance plans that cover abortions. The rules apply to all federal agencies and programs, including those in Washington DC. There are three exceptions where federal money can be used: when the pregnancy is from rape or incest, or when the mother's life is in danger. The bill also stops federal employees from performing abortions at government health centers. Health insurance companies must clearly explain to customers if their plans cover abortions and how much extra it costs.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on the Judiciary, and Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for co

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title; Table of Contents
sections.text: This law is called the No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion and Abortion Insurance Full Disclosure Act of two thousand twenty-five. The law has two main parts. Title One stops the federal government from using tax money to pay for abortions. Title Two explains how this applies to the Affordable Care Act, including rules about premium credits and requirements to tell people about abortion coverage.

sections.title: Section 101 Prohibiting Taxpayer Funded Abortions
sections.text: This section adds a new chapter to federal law called Prohibiting Taxpayer Funded Abortions. Section three hundred one says no federal money can be spent on any abortion. Section three hundred two says no federal money can be spent on health insurance plans that cover abortions. Section three hundred three says federal hospitals and federal government doctors cannot perform abortions. Section three hundred four says people, organizations, states, and cities can still buy separate abortion coverage using only their own money, not federal money. Section three hundred five says private insurance companies can still offer abortion coverage as long as only private money pays for it. Section three hundred six says this new law does not change any other federal laws that already limit abortion funding. Section three hundred seven says this law does not apply to treating medical problems caused by abortions. Section three hundred eight says the ban on federal funding does not apply to abortions when the pregnancy was caused by rape or incest, or when the woman's life is in danger. Section three hundred nine says these rules also apply to Washington D.C.

sections.title: Section 102 Amendment to Table of Chapters
sections.text: This section adds the new chapter about prohibiting taxpayer funded abortions to the official list of chapters in federal law.

sections.title: Section 201 Clarifying Application of Prohibition to Premium Credits and Cost-sharing Reductions Under ACA
sections.text: This section changes tax law to say people cannot get government help paying for health insurance if their plan covers abortions, except for cases of rape, incest, or danger to the mother's life. People can still buy separate abortion coverage with their own money, but the government will not help pay for it. Insurance companies can still offer these separate plans, but no government money can be used. The section also says small businesses cannot get tax credits for health plans that cover abortions, but they can buy separate abortion coverage for their workers using only their own money. These changes apply to tax years ending after December thirty-first, two thousand twenty-five. The rules also apply to multi-state health plans sold on government insurance exchanges.

sections.title: Section 202 Revision of Notice Requirements Regarding Disclosure of Extent of Health Plan Coverage of Abortion and Abortion Premium Surcharges
sections.text: This section requires health insurance plans to clearly tell people about any abortion coverage when they sign up. The information must be easy to see in all advertising materials, comparison tools, and benefit summaries. If a health plan covers abortions and charges extra for this coverage, the plan must show this extra charge separately and clearly identify it. These new disclosure rules take effect thirty days after this law is passed.
